meaning: n.不恰当,离题,不相干的事物
examples: The fear of irrelevancy will haunt European leaders in 2010.
But stretch a topic too far and usually it will collapse into irrelevancy.
That I am trapped in this particular irrelevancy is never more apparent to me than when I am home.
Fortunately, Bitcoin transcends the conventional philosophy of regulation by reducing certain aspects to irrelevancy.
Ezra Klein has a column up urging unions to reform or fade into irrelevancy.
